title: KALIA Yojana (Odisha) — ₹12,500 for Farmers
meta_description: KALIA Yojana Odisha 2026 — ₹12,500/year for small farmers, landless labourers. Updated under CM Kisan. Check status at kfrionline.in.

hero.one_liner: Odisha's direct income support for small farmers (₹5,000/season) and landless agricultural households — now transitioning to CM Kisan Yojana with expanded coverage

what_is_it: KALIA (Krushak Assistance for Livelihood and Income Augmentation) is Odisha's flagship farmer welfare scheme launched in December 2018 by the BJD government. The scheme has three main components: (1) ₹5,000 per season for small/marginal farmers to cover cultivation costs, (2) ₹12,500 annually for landless agricultural households for livestock and allied activities, (3) ₹10,000/year for vulnerable agricultural households. Over 57 lakh families currently benefit from KALIA across Odisha.

The scheme uniquely targets small and marginal farmers (owning up to 5 acres) and landless agricultural workers — the most economically vulnerable segments in rural Odisha. Large farmers exceeding 5 acres are deliberately excluded, making KALIA more progressive and means-tested than universal schemes like PM Kisan. Unlike PM Kisan which reaches all farmers regardless of farm size, KALIA's vertical targeting addresses the poorest sections first.

Beneficiary identification uses a transparent, district-level database system. The complete beneficiary list is publicly accessible at kfrionline.in where anyone can verify names, payment history, and status by searching across district, block, Gram Panchayat, and village levels. Direct Aadhaar-linked bank transfers eliminate middlemen and rent-seeking by local officials.

KALIA and PM Kisan work complementarily, not competitively. An eligible Odisha farmer owning 3 acres can simultaneously receive ₹6,000/year from PM Kisan (Central) plus ₹5,000/season from KALIA (State) — totaling ₹11,000-16,000 annually. Landless workers receiving KALIA cannot access PM Kisan since the latter requires land ownership, but KALIA's ₹12,500 livestock component provides viable income alternatives. This dualistic structure has made Odisha a model for supplementary state-level schemes.

eligibility.label: Small/Marginal Farmers
eligibility.value: Owning up to 5 acres — get ₹5,000 per season (₹25,000 over 5 seasons) for cultivation, seeds, fertilizer, labour

eligibility.label: Landless Households
eligibility.value: Agricultural labourers with zero land — get ₹12,500 for livestock (goat, poultry, fish, duck) or mushroom cultivation

eligibility.label: Vulnerable Households
eligibility.value: Elderly, disabled, chronically ill farmers unable to cultivate — get ₹10,000/year for sustenance and medical needs

eligibility.label: Life Insurance
eligibility.value: ALL KALIA beneficiaries receive ₹2 lakh life insurance cover + ₹2 lakh accidental death/disability at zero premium

eligibility.label: Exclusion Criteria
eligibility.value: Large farmers (>5 acres), income tax payers, government employees, professionals (doctors, lawyers, engineers, chartered accountants)

eligibility.label: Verification Method
eligibility.value: Visit kfrionline.in → Beneficiary List → select your district, block, Gram Panchayat, village → search by name or Aadhaar

extra_sections.heading: KALIA vs PM Kisan vs CM Kisan — Understanding Odisha's Farmer Support
Recent Scheme Evolution — KALIA to CM Kisan Transition
content: In 2026, the Odisha government introduced CM Kisan Yojana to complement or potentially replace KALIA over time. CM Kisan provides ₹4,000 annually for small/marginal farmers in two installments (₹2,000 each).
Landless households continue receiving ₹12,500 under the landless component.
Current status: KALIA remains active and operational. If you were enrolled under KALIA before, you continue receiving KALIA benefits unless officially migrated to CM Kisan.
Payments are made around festivals (Akshaya Tritiya in April, during harvest seasons). Always verify your status at kfrionline.in before the cropping season.
The government has NOT issued a blanket closure notice for KALIA. Given the massive beneficiary base (57+ lakh families), a sudden discontinuation would be politically and socially untenable.
Both schemes may coexist or KALIA benefits may be absorbed into CM Kisan gradually. Either way, small farmers in Odisha remain covered.

Payment schedule: KALIA payments are seasonal — linked to kharif (monsoon, July-Oct) and rabi (winter, Oct-Mar) cropping cycles. Exact payment dates are announced by the district administration, usually aligning with cultural festivals to maximize accessibility.

extra_sections.heading: Common Reasons for Payment Delay or Rejection
content: Aadhaar not linked to bank account: Visit your bank with Aadhaar card and passbook. Request Aadhaar linking (free service). Takes 2-3 days.
Bank account inactive: If no transaction in 6+ months, banks can freeze accounts as per RBI rules. Deposit ₹1 using mobile app or visit bank counter to reactivate.

Name mismatch between KALIA database and Aadhaar: Government spells 'Ramakrishnan' as 'Ramkrishnan' — data entry errors. Visit Gram Panchayat office with Aadhaar and land documents to correct the spelling.

Land record updated after enrollment: If you bought additional land or your property was subdivided, you might now exceed 5-acre limit. Verify your land holdings at the district revenue office before the next payment.
Payment already credited but you didn't receive it: Some bank-side issues delay crediting even after government transmits funds. Wait 5-7 days and check with your bank's helpline.
NEFT/RTGS transfers occasionally have clearing delays.

how_to_apply.title: Check if your name is already in the beneficiary list
how_to_apply.description: Visit kfrionline.in → Beneficiary List section → select your district, then block, then Gram Panchayat, then village → search your name or Aadhaar. If already listed, skip to Step 4.

how_to_apply.title: If not listed, apply through Gram Panchayat with documents
how_to_apply.description: Visit your village Gram Panchayat office with: land documents (patta, jami parcha, or loan agreement), Aadhaar card, bank passbook, and 10th or 12th mark sheet (proof of age). No application fee.

how_to_apply.title: Block office verifies and adds your entry to database
how_to_apply.description: Gram Panchayat submits your application to Block Development Officer (BDO). Verification includes land record cross-checking with district revenue office and household income assessment. Processing: 30-60 days. You'll receive a confirmation letter from the block office.

how_to_apply.title: Monitor payment status and receive funds
how_to_apply.description: Once enrolled, log into kfrionline.in with your Aadhaar to track payment status. Amount is automatically transferred to your Aadhaar-linked bank account on payment dates (announced seasonally). In case of delay, visit block office with confirmation letter.

important_alert.text: KALIA scheme status is under government review. While active in March 2026, always verify your current benefit entitlements at kfrionline.in or call 1800-345-6789. Some districts may be transitioning to CM Kisan Yojana — check your block office for latest announcements.

important_dates.label: Beneficiary List Updates
important_dates.value: Quarterly — check before each season at kfrionline.in

faqs.question: I'm a small farmer with 4 acres. How much total do I get annually?
faqs.answer: As a small farmer (<5 acres), you get ₹5,000 per cropping season. With two main seasons (kharif and rabi) yearly, that's ₹10,000/year from KALIA. Plus ₹6,000/year from PM Kisan if enrolled. Total: ₹16,000/year. Additionally, you receive ₹2 lakh life insurance + ₹2 lakh accidental cover at zero premium under KALIA. This is one of India's most generous farmer support packages for marginal farmers.

faqs.question: My name was in the beneficiary list but my payment didn't arrive. What went wrong?
faqs.answer: Most common reasons: (1) Aadhaar not linked to your bank account — visit your bank, (2) Bank account inactive — make one transaction to reactivate, (3) Name spelling mismatch between KALIA and Aadhaar — name is 'Murli' in KALIA but 'Murali' in Aadhaar — go to GP office to correct, (4) Your land extent increased beyond 5 acres — you become ineligible, verify at revenue office. Contact block office with your beneficiary ID for assistance.

faqs.question: I'm a landless agricultural laborer. What exactly can I do with ₹12,500?
faqs.answer: KALIA's ₹12,500 for landless households is specifically for starting allied activities: (1) Goat rearing — buy 5-8 goats, breed them, sell milk/meat, (2) Poultry — buy 100-150 chicks, grow to adult, sell eggs/meat, (3) Fish culture — lease 1-2 acre ponds, stock fingerlings, harvest, (4) Duck farming — similar to poultry, (5) Mushroom cultivation — indoor farming on ₹5-8K worth of structure, harvest 4-6 times/year. The amount covers startup costs. Income from these activities can be ₹2,000-5,000/month once established. Government also provides training and input subsidies in some districts — check with block office.

faqs.question: Is KALIA scheme shutting down? I heard CM Kisan is the new scheme.
faqs.answer: As of March 2026, KALIA remains operational with 57+ lakh active beneficiaries. The government introduced CM Kisan Yojana to provide additional/supplementary support. Both schemes are currently parallel. Rumors of KALIA closure have circulated since 2023, but no official order discontinuing it has been published. Given the massive beneficiary base, abrupt closure is unlikely. Stay updated at kfrionline.in and your block office. If migration to CM Kisan is announced, it will be through official government notification.

faqs.question: Can I check my KALIA payment history online?
faqs.answer: Yes. Visit kfrionline.in → Payment Status → enter your Aadhaar or KALIA ID. You'll see: (1) Enrollment date, (2) Seasons for which you received payment, (3) Exact amounts credited, (4) Payment dates. You can download statements for bank reconciliation or government office verification. If there's a discrepancy (payment shows credited but you didn't receive), save this printout and approach your bank's grievance cell.

faqs.question: I have 6 acres. Am I eligible?
faqs.answer: No. KALIA eligibility is strictly for farmers owning UP TO 5 acres. If you have 6 acres, you're ineligible regardless of actual farm productivity or income. This size-based exclusion is intentional — the scheme targets small and marginal farmers specifically. However, you're eligible for PM Kisan (₹6,000/year) if enrolled. Also, if you're in the vulnerable category (age >65, disability >40%, chronic illness), you might qualify for the sustenance component of KALIA under different criteria — check with your GP office.

faqs.question: What's the difference between KALIA and PM Kisan?
faqs.answer: PM Kisan: Central scheme, covers ALL farmers with any land size, ₹6,000/year, no livestock component. KALIA: Odisha state scheme, covers small farmers <5 acres, ₹5,000/season (₹20,000 over 5 seasons), includes landless component (₹12,500), includes life insurance. KALIA is more generous but state-limited. PM Kisan is uniform nationwide but less targeted. An Odisha farmer can receive both simultaneously.

ministry: Department of Agriculture and Farmers Empowerment, Government of Odisha
